neighborhoods.name: North Loop / Mill District
neighborhoods.description: Converted flour mill district with restaurants, art galleries, and the riverfront Gold Medal Park
neighborhoods.character: Mill district, flour heritage, riverfront dining

housing.predominantType: Converted mill lofts, condos, and modern apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Minneapolis. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Edina / St. Louis Park
neighborhoods.description: First-ring suburbs with shopping, dining, and established tree-lined residential neighborhoods
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Minneapolis's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Brooklyn Park / Brooklyn Center
neighborhoods.description: Northern suburbs with diverse communities, affordable housing, and improving transit
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Eden Prairie / Minnetonka
neighborhoods.description: Western suburbs with top schools, lake recreation, and extensive trail systems

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes near lakes and parks

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
